Tests if dividing NaN by NaN results in NaN using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of dividing `Number.NaN` by `Number.NaN` in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of this operation is `NaN`, which is confirmed by the `isNaN` function returning `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles the division of `NaN` values according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on validating the behavior of `NaN` division.
